name: stripe
tagline: The API is forever — type the code incrementally, review the interface exhaustively, never break users.
philosophy: External integrators must never break. Public surface changes get design review distinct from code review; type coverage only ratchets up; docs travel with the API.

gates.description: Conditional on existing type tooling: no file moves down a strictness level, and the diff's type coverage never decreases. New files start strict.

Any change to the public surface (routes, exported symbols, response shapes, OpenAPI) requires a short design note from the builder covering naming, consistency with existing conventions, and versioning.
Breaking changes to a public contract must be versioned or flagged; removing/renaming a public field without a migration path blocks GREEN.
Public-surface changes without corresponding doc/OpenAPI updates are findings.
